# Questions in Livestream 71

Live Stream #71: The First of Octember

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=313" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:13</a> | Why does the Bible speak about physical endings but not physical origins, and does that make full preterism plausible? | He rejected the premise, arguing that Genesis and eschatology are framed more by purpose and teleology than by bare physical description, and he treated full preterism as outside historic Christian orthodoxy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:34</a> | When is his discussion with Titus Kennedy, and has it already happened? | He said it was still upcoming and described it as a serious discussion on the date of the Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=781" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:01</a> | Will he bring popcorn to the Titus Kennedy interview? | Probably not. He wanted the exchange to stay serious rather than turn into a spectacle. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=1230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:30</a> | Is Christianity surviving, becoming accepted, and then dominant in Rome prophetically significant, perhaps foreshadowed by Daniel 7? | He said that reading is plausible, especially in light of Daniel 7's holy ones receiving the kingdom, though other scholars may differ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=1407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:27</a> | Are there similarities between the feasts in Leviticus and Egyptian festivals? | He said Egyptian festival practice is hard to reconstruct and differs from Leviticus in priestly and communal participation, though there are broader ritual similarities such as purity practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=1729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:49</a> | How can a futurist handle New Testament time statements that seem to demand a soon coming? | He said `soon` can mean imminent or sudden rather than necessarily within a short calendar interval, which is how he thinks most futurist exegetes handle the issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=1890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:30</a> | What is his opinion on the twelve tribes of Israel, Gilead in Judges 5:17, Simeon, and the claim that the twelve tribes are a myth? | He rejected the myth claim, said Gilead was not an Israelite tribe, and argued that some tribes or groups are included or excluded in lists because of their obligations in particular contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=2306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:26</a> | What is his opinion of the Hebrew Roots movement and Black Hebrew Israelites? | He treated them as very different movements, criticizing Black Hebrew Israelite claims about lineage and race while describing Hebrew Roots as a legalistic movement that tends toward works-based salvation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=2828" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:08</a> | Does a new clan branching off mean the older clan can still continue? | Yes. He said a parent clade can persist for centuries after a new clade or clan branches from it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=2935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:55</a> | What does he think about dating Job to the seventh to fourth century BC based on Arabic and Edomite words? | He said Job could plausibly fall in a broad range, roughly from the tenth to fifth century BC, and that Arabic and Edomite vocabulary does not force a late date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=3256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:16</a> | Since Genesis mentions Ephron the Hittite, is there evidence for Hittites in Canaan during Abraham's time? | He said direct evidence for Hittites in Abraham's time is lacking, but early Hittite history is shadowy enough that their presence cannot be ruled out. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=3506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:26</a> | Does Deuteronomy as a whole follow the structure of Hittite treaties, even if there were multiple redactions? | He accepted the Hittite-treaty structure but was skeptical of redaction criticism as usually framed, allowing for updated language, spelling, interpretive glosses, and stitched-together sections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=3760" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:40</a> | Did the worship of Yahweh have Egyptian origins, and is there evidence for the divine name before the first millennium BC? | He said Yahweh worship does not seem Egyptian in origin and is more likely Midianite, while the Shasu of Yhw inscription may be relevant but is not certain proof. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=4026" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:06</a> | What is his opinion on the Yu-Gi-Oh pharaoh question? | The question wording is unclear in the transcript. Once he understood it was about Yu-Gi-Oh, he said he had not watched it yet and could not really answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=4262" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:02</a> | In Deuteronomy 23:7, were the Hyksos already gone, and were the Egyptians native Egyptians rather than Hyksos? | He said the Hyksos were long gone by then, were a ruling group rather than an ethnicity, and that Deuteronomy's Egyptian reference is not to the Hyksos. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=4535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:35</a> | Is the man with six fingers and toes in 2 Samuel 21:20 a cultural idiom or literal? | He said it was probably literal, but carried a Philistine or Canaanite cultural meaning tied to disruptive magic and supernatural favor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=4912" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:52</a> | Did gnosticism begin after Christianity, or is it an older belief system that used Christianity to allegorize its doctrines? | He said gnosticism seems older than Christianity, arising from Egyptian mystery religion mixed with Platonism and later Jewish and Christian symbols. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=5465" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:05</a> | What is his opinion on cousin marriages in the Bible, and are they significant, right, or wrong today? | He said biblical kinship terms are broader than modern cousin language, that cousin marriage may be permitted but is not beneficial, especially because of genetic concerns with close cousin marriages. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=5877" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:57</a> | Would it have been unusual for Genesis's original audience to say that women were created in the image of God? | No. He said Genesis 1 explicitly includes male and female in the image of God, while Genesis 2 also lets women be understood as related to man. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=6111" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:51</a> | What does he think of the idea that Christ was based on pagan myth, especially Horus? | He rejected it strongly, saying Horus does not die and rise like Christ and that serious scholars do not support the claimed parallel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=6427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:07</a> | Is Titus Kennedy better than Scott Stripling? | He said they may be different and that both may be good field archaeologists, but the real question is how well Kennedy will do as a historian in the discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=6571" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:31</a> | How complicated were Egyptian lines of succession, and were they like modern monarchies that search the family tree for a senior heir? | He said Egyptian succession was simpler: sons of the great queen came first, then sons of minor queens, then daughters, and after the royal harem was exhausted a succession crisis often produced a new dynasty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=7088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:08</a> | If the Hyksos invited Israel into Egypt, does that make Deuteronomy 23:7's reason for not detesting Egyptians invalid? | He said no. Even if the Hyksos invited them, the Israelites still sojourned on Egyptian land after native Egyptians controlled it, so the reason still works. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=7598" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:38</a> | Has `Allah` always meant `God`, or is it a pagan divine name? | He said it is the generic Arabic Semitic word for God, used even by Arabic Christians, and not the name of a pagan deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=7864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:04</a> | What did the cherubim in the temple and on the Ark look like, and were they like Ezekiel's vision or Egyptian in style? | He said they were probably Egyptian in style, likely women with wings for arms, and not like the later cherubim of Ezekiel's vision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=8089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:49</a> | What is his opinion of the deuterocanon / apocrypha? | He said those books can provide useful historical context and literary value, but he does not regard them as Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=8352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:12</a> | When did gnosticism become entangled with Christianity? | He placed the major entanglement in the second century AD, when Gnostic gospels and strong Christian responses to them become visible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=8654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:14</a> | What is his take on Wisdom 2:10-20 as Jesus prophecy, and could the deuterocanon be sub-inspired? | He said he would need to look again at Wisdom, but rejected the idea of sub-inspiration: a text is either inspired or not, though it can still be useful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=9005" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:05</a> | Is Moses's use of water in Genesis 1 a reference to deity? | He said water can symbolize divine realities elsewhere, but he did not think Genesis 1 requires that reading; there it is more about creation being ordered and given purpose. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=9547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:07</a> | Were the gnostics persecuted alongside Christians, and why would they want to be tied to an illegal religion? | He said the Romans generally did not target gnostics like Christians because gnostics could accommodate Roman religious expectations and were not bound to Christian confession in the same way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=9856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:16</a> | Is there a book he recommends on biblical numerology? | No. He affirmed that some biblical numbers are symbolically important, especially `7`, `12`, and `40`, but said most numerology books overextend the evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=10130" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:50</a> | Was Moses really 120 when he died, or are the sets of 40 used to mark stages of his life? | He favored the symbolic reading, saying 40 is a common idiom for an unspecified long time and that Moses was probably not literally 120 in this reconstruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=10423" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:43</a> | If the Pentateuch has references written from the perspective of someone in Canaan, does that mean Moses could not be the author? | He said neither extreme is right: Moses did not write every word, but later editorial interpolation and glosses do not mean zero Mosaic authorship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=11371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:31</a> | What does he think of Paul Copan's books on God and Old Testament moral problems? | He had not read the books, but said Copan's speaking on the subject sounded mostly sound and thorough, though he disagreed with some solutions such as the minority view on Jephthah's daughter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6bP2Ikr9Xs?t=11844" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:24</a> | Bach versus Beethoven: who would he put his money on? | Jokingly and musically, he picked Bach, saying Bach adapts well to a single instrument while Beethoven usually needs a large orchestra to shine. |
